task_completion.description: Connect to the SeisComP database using the provided sysop credentials.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal or database client showing a successful connection to the 'seiscomp' database using the username 'sysop'.

task_completion.description: Extract the 2024 Noto Peninsula earthquake data from the database.
task_completion.visual_evidence: SQL query results visible on screen showing data for the Noto Peninsula earthquake (e.g., origin time in early 2024, magnitude, coordinates, and depth).

task_completion.description: Create the KML file at the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, terminal, or file manager showing the creation or existence of the file exactly at '/home/ga/earthquake_catalog.kml'.

task_completion.description: Include a Document element with the correct name in the KML file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The contents of the KML file showing a <Document> tag containing a <name> tag with the exact text 'SeisComP Earthquake Catalog'.

task_completion.description: Include a Placemark with the event's precise coordinates and origin time in ISO 8601 format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The KML file contents showing a <Placemark> tag that contains a <Point> with <coordinates> (longitude, latitude) and a time element (like <TimeStamp><when>) formatted in ISO 8601 (e.g., YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ).

task_completion.description: Include the event's depth (in km) and magnitude within the Placemark.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The KML file contents showing the depth and magnitude values associated with the earthquake, typically within <ExtendedData> or <description> tags inside the <Placemark>.

integrity.description: The agent must extract the data from the local SeisComP database, not use external web search or hallucinate the values.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show a database query being executed (e.g., via terminal mysql/psql or a DB GUI) that returns the earthquake data used in the KML.

integrity.description: The KML file must be saved exactly at the requested path.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file path '/home/ga/earthquake_catalog.kml' is explicitly visible in the editor's title bar, save dialog, or terminal prompt.

integrity.description: The output must be formatted as valid XML/KML.
integrity.visual_evidence: The file contents show proper XML/KML syntax with opening and closing tags (e.g., <?xml...>, <kml>, <Document>, <Placemark>), rather than just a plain text list of values.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file is exactly '/home/ga/earthquake_catalog.kml'. The database name is 'seiscomp' and the credentials are user 'sysop' with password 'sysop'. The KML file must contain a Document with the exact name 'SeisComP Earthquake Catalog'. The event is the 2024 Noto Peninsula earthquake (USGS event us6000m0xl).
